task_completion.description: The agent opens the Visit Note form for the patient.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A clinical form titled 'Visit Note' (or similar encounter form) is open and visible in the OpenMRS interface.

task_completion.description: The agent sets the Primary Diagnosis to 'Headache'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The word 'Headache' is typed, searched, or selected in the 'Primary Diagnosis' or 'Diagnosis' input field.

task_completion.description: The agent does not add any other observations or notes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: All other text areas, observation fields, and secondary diagnosis fields in the Visit Note form are left completely blank.

task_completion.description: The agent clicks the button to save the form.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The cursor is seen clicking a 'Save', 'Submit', or 'Enter' button at the end of the Visit Note form.

task_completion.description: The Visit Note is successfully saved to the patient's record.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A success toast/notification appears (e.g., 'Saved successfully'), or the newly created 'Visit Note' is visible in the patient's recent encounters or visits list.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the OpenMRS graphical user interface in a web browser.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show a standard web browser navigating the OpenMRS EMR system, rather than a terminal window executing API scripts or database commands.

integrity.description: The agent did not fake the task completion in a text editor or dummy page.
integrity.visual_evidence: The inputs are entered into the actual OpenMRS web application fields, not a notepad, text editor, or a locally modified HTML file.

integrity.description: The agent recorded the note for the patient originally displayed on the screen.
integrity.visual_evidence: The patient name/ID at the top of the screen or dashboard remains consistent with the patient shown at the beginning of the trajectory.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires creating a new encounter in the OpenMRS application. The encounter type must be exactly 'Visit Note' and the primary diagnosis must be exactly 'Headache'.
